etymology.description: 穴(穴, "cave") + 目(目, "eye") + 支(支, "hand holding a stick") = authority asserted -> eminent
etymology.interpretation: The combination of a cave or closed space with an eye suggests observation or vigilance, while the hand holding a stick implies authority or guidance. Together, these motifs imply a sense of prominence or eminence, as a figure who oversees and controls from a place of power.
